notable_pick_trades: Traded David Montgomery to HOU for 4th-round pick (#128), Juice Scruggs, and 2027 7th. Traded both 2026 3rd-round picks to JAX in 2025 draft to move up for WR Isaac TeSlaa. Projected 5th-round compensatory pick.
roster_context: Detroit went 9-8 in 2025 and missed the playoffs after back-to-back division titles. The offensive line regressed badly after Frank Ragnow's retirement, the secondary was decimated by injuries (Kerby Joseph knee, Brian Branch torn Achilles, Terrion Arnold shoulder surgery + legal issues), and the team still lacks a reliable EDGE2 opposite Aidan Hutchinson. The Lions are in cap trouble (~$12M over the cap pre-moves) but remain in their Super Bowl window with a loaded core of Goff, St. Brown, Gibbs, Sewell, Hutchinson, and McNeill. New OC Drew Petzing brings a run-heavy, multi-TE approach. GM Brad Holmes must thread the needle of competing now while rebuilding depth.

needs.explanation: The Lions' biggest defensive need. Al-Quadin Muhammad (11 sacks in 2025, career year) and Marcus Davenport (1 sack, injured again) are both UFAs. Tyrus Wheat was not tendered. Behind Aidan Hutchinson, only Josh Paschal (injured, unproductive in 4 years), Tyler Lacy (minimal pass rush), and Ahmed Hassanein (missed 2025) remain under contract. Detroit needs a true three-down starter who can rush the passer AND set the edge in the run game opposite Hutchinson. The 2026 EDGE draft class is deep, making this a prime first-round target.
needs.depth: Josh Paschal, Tyler Lacy, Ahmed Hassanein — extremely thin; no proven starter opposite Hutchinson
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 priority — prospects like Cashius Howell, Keldric Faulk, Akheem Mesidor, or Zion Young

needs.explanation: The Lions lost both Roy Lopez (2yr/$11.5M to Arizona) and DJ Reader (still unsigned/likely departing) without adding a single interior defensive lineman. Tyleik Williams (2025 1st-round pick) is the only reliable interior piece. Detroit's run defense anchor is gone. IDL should be a Day 2 draft priority and still a free-agency target.

Taylor Decker announced his return for an 11th season but dealt with a shoulder injury all year (missed some practices) and turns 33 in August. Dan Campbell explicitly said 'even if he returns, we still need to find a guy' at tackle. Swing tackle Dan Skipper retired and joined the coaching staff. Depth behind Decker and Sewell is barren — Giovanni Manu is still developing. The Lions need a future LT who can serve as OT3/swing tackle in 2026 and potentially start in 2027.
needs.current_starter: Taylor Decker (LT, returning but aging/injury concerns), Penei Sewell (RT, All-Pro, locked up)
needs.depth: Giovanni Manu (raw, developmental), Juice Scruggs (interior flexibility) — Dan Skipper retired
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — strong OT draft class; could develop behind Decker

needs.explanation: Both starting safeties are coming off significant injuries with uncertain timelines. Kerby Joseph (chronic knee condition, played only 6 games in 2025, described his knee as 'messed up') has no firm return date — Dan Campbell said it's 'tough to say' if he'll be available. Brian Branch tore his Achilles in December and won't be back until mid-season at the earliest. Avonte Maddox, who filled in admirably (79.1 PFF grade), is a UFA. Thomas Harper showed promise but is unproven long-term. Dan Jackson missed his entire rookie year.
needs.current_starter: Kerby Joseph (injured, uncertain return), Brian Branch (Achilles, likely out until October)
needs.depth: Thomas Harper (ERFA, 9 starts), Dan Jackson (2nd-year, missed rookie season) — Avonte Maddox (UFA, critical to re-sign)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 for young developmental safety; also need veteran FA signing for insurance

needs.explanation: Jack Campbell is a foundational piece (5th-year option for 2027, extension expected). Derrick Barnes is signed through 2027. Alex Anzalone is a UFA and likely to depart, along with Malcolm Rodriguez, Grant Stuard, and Zach Cunningham. The starters are solid but LB depth needs replenishing, particularly for special teams contributors.
needs.depth: Ben Nowaske (ERFA, special teams), Ahmed Hassanein — Anzalone (UFA), Rodriguez (UFA)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 5-7 or low-cost FA for depth and special teams

needs.explanation: David Montgomery was traded to Houston, leaving Jahmyr Gibbs as the clear lead back. Gibbs is elite but needs a complementary back to manage his workload and prevent wear. The Lions used a formal Combine meeting on Penn State RB Singleton, signaling interest in a Day 3 RB. Craig Reynolds is a depth piece but not a long-term RB2 answer.
needs.current_starter: Jahmyr Gibbs (Pro Bowl, extension talks beginning)
needs.depth: Craig Reynolds — no proven RB2 after Montgomery trade
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4-5 — complementary back to spell Gibbs

needs.explanation: Secondary is in disarray. Terrion Arnold (2024 1st-round pick) is under legal cloud after being named in a Florida court order related to armed robbery/kidnapping — even if not charged, potential NFL suspension looms. Arnold also had shoulder surgery ending his 2025 after 8 games. DJ Reed underperformed after a hamstring injury and has much to prove. Ennis Rakestraw missed all of 2025 with a shoulder injury. Nickel CB Amik Robertson and depth piece Rock Ya-Sin are both UFAs. The Arnold situation is causing the Lions to potentially pivot offseason plans.
needs.current_starter: DJ Reed (outside), Terrion Arnold (outside, legal/injury uncertainty), Ennis Rakestraw (returning from injury)
needs.depth: Carlton Dorsey, Isaiah Whiteside — Amik Robertson (UFA, nickel), Rock Ya-Sin (UFA)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 — need nickel and depth; could become Round 1 need if Arnold situation worsens

needs.explanation: Alim McNeill is locked up as the 3-tech and Tyleik Williams (2025 1st-round pick) is expected to step into a bigger role at nose tackle. However, both DJ Reader and Roy Lopez are UFAs. Reader never returned to his prior form, while Lopez had a career year and is a strong re-sign candidate. If Lopez departs, the DT rotation behind McNeill and Williams is thin. Levi Onwuzurike has been perpetually injured.
needs.current_starter: Alim McNeill (3-tech, under contract), Tyleik Williams (NT, expanding role)
needs.depth: Levi Onwuzurike (UFA, constantly injured), Pat Wingo — DJ Reader (UFA), Roy Lopez (UFA, priority re-sign)

needs.explanation: Center is a glaring hole. Frank Ragnow retired before 2025, Graham Glasgow replaced him but struggled (PFF grade 56.8, ranked 33rd of 40 centers) and has been released. The Lions acquired Juice Scruggs in the Montgomery trade but he is not expected to start. Tate Ratledge (2025 2nd-round pick, started 17 games at RG as a rookie) is an option to move to center, but that would leave a hole at guard. Christian Mahogany started 11 games at LG but has injury history. Interior OL inconsistency hurt both pass protection and the run game in 2025.
needs.current_starter: Tate Ratledge (RG), Christian Mahogany (LG) — Center: VACANT after Glasgow release
needs.depth: Juice Scruggs (acquired via trade, backup caliber), Seth McLaughlin (futures contract)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 for center; could also address via free agency (Linderbaum, McGovern, Mays linked)

needs.explanation: The TE room was devastated by injuries in 2025 — Sam LaPorta (back), Brock Wright (throat), and Shane Zylstra all finished on IR. LaPorta is expected back but new OC Drew Petzing's system emphasizes multi-TE packages heavily. Wright is under contract but has durability concerns. Zylstra and Firkser are UFAs. A Day 3 pass-catching TE who can also block would fit the scheme.
needs.current_starter: Sam LaPorta (expected back from back injury), Brock Wright
needs.depth: James Mitchell (futures), Parker Hesse — Zylstra and Firkser are UFAs
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4-6 — deep TE draft class; Petzing's scheme elevates TE value

free_agency_impact: Signed Cade Mays (IOL) from Carolina Panthers — $8.3M AAV
Signed Juice Scruggs (IOL) from Houston Texans — $1.6M AAV
Signed Larry Borom (OT) from Miami Dolphins — $2.5M AAV
Signed Jake Bates (K) from Detroit Lions — $1.0M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: Detroit runs a 4-3 base defense under DC Kelvin Sheppard. EDGE rushers must be big-bodied (6'4+, 260+) pocket collapsers who can set the edge against the run — speed rushers alone won't fit. The Lions prefer versatile edges who can kick inside on passing downs. On offense, new OC Drew Petzing runs a physical, run-heavy scheme with heavy multi-TE personnel groupings. IOL and OT prospects must be powerful run blockers who fit zone concepts. The Lions value 'football character' — high-motor, tough, culture-fit players — very highly in their evaluation process.
